name: Emerald International Bus
recent_news: No public news, M&A, or web footprint located; not visibly associated with Springfield/Eugene-area school districts or LTD. Likely a very small operator or possibly a charter/tour micro-business at 3255 Gateway St.
fit_notes: Too small and opaque to assess; no confirmation it is a yellow-bus school contractor. Likely de minimis to Summit unless on-the-ground diligence reveals district contracts.

name: Simon Schools Trnsprtn LLC
recent_news: No press coverage, M&A activity, or independent web footprint located. Lynn Public Schools transportation is primarily routed via the district and contractor NRT Bus; Simon's role, if any, is not publicly documented.
fit_notes: Small family-owned LLC named after principal Harold Simon; in MA where Beacon Mobility/NRT consolidation is active. Plausible micro tuck-in but needs phone diligence; risk that it is a sub-route operator or has already been folded into Beacon.

name: Healthy School Bus LLC
recent_news: No web footprint, news, or M&A activity located. The Eucalyptus Ave address maps to Stoneridge Towne Centre retail; likely a mailing/registered-agent address rather than a yard. Moreno Valley USD uses its own large EV fleet and First Student, not a small contractor.
fit_notes: Unclear whether this is an active yellow-bus contractor or a non-emissions/EV consultancy using 'Healthy School Bus' branding. Low conviction tuck-in; needs CA Sec. of State and CHP SPAB certification check before pursuing.

name: City Bus & Truck Inc
recent_news: No evidence of operating as a school-bus contractor. Arab City Schools self-operates its student transportation. Name and location (US-231 commercial corridor) suggest a bus/truck dealership, repair, or freight operator rather than a school-bus contractor; AL school transportation is overwhelmingly district-operated.
fit_notes: Likely NOT a student-transportation operator. Alabama districts run buses in-house, and the name/address pattern fits a dealer/repair shop. Recommend removing from tuck-in list pending confirmation.

name: Washington County Mini Bus Inc
owner_or_parent: Nonprofit / public-funded (ECICOG / Washington County, IA / IA DOT / FTA)
recent_news: FY2025 revenue ~$1.12M (up from $832k FY2024), funded by city/county/state/federal transit dollars; operates as a 501(c)(3) public transit provider, not a school-bus contractor. Washington Community School District separately runs its own 10-bus school fleet.
fit_notes: NOT an acquirable target - 501(c)(3) public-transit nonprofit funded by FTA/state grants, not a private school-bus contractor. Exclude from Summit tuck-in pipeline.
